summary: Solid soundboard capture with a rough first half but a standout second half featuring Black Peter and an epic Morning Dew with Stephen Stills.
review: The recording is a decent‑to‑good soundboard source that sounds clear in the latter portion, though the early set feels a bit muddy and uneven. Musically the show shines after the midway point – Jerry Garcia and guest Stephen Stills trade jug‑band style riffs on Black Peter and deliver a three‑dimensional Morning Dew that many reviewers cite as the highlight.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are 4‑5 stars, praising the performance and noting only minor recording roughness; the strong second half outweighs early imperfections.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally nice and clear sound, especially in the latter part; early tracks described as rough/ugly with some intrusive elements but no major drop‑outs or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Early half feels uneven and vocal‑heavy, but the band gels in the second half – Stephen Stills adds a wild edge and the group delivers a memorable Morning Dew.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for jamming with Stephen Stills in a jug‑band style; his guitar work is highlighted as snarling and stinging.
